notes: DOCUMENTED TARGET — not yet recordable (driver gap: gap:slash-command; see assessment.json). opencode ships /undo + /redo with git-snapshot semantics: /undo rewinds the conversation to a previous message AND restores the working-tree files, REMOVING the subsequent messages from the SQLite session — an IN-PLACE rewind on the SAME ses_… (the daemon's ProcessOwnedStore source reads the same session id throughout), not a fork like codex. So the observable arc is: session born ready → turn 1 (ready→working→ready) → turn 2 (ready→working→ready) → user rewinds to the turn-1 checkpoint via /undo (the session stays ready — a rewind is not a turn; the store row shrinks as messages are removed, but no state change distinguishes the rewind) → turn 3 from the rewound point (ready→working→ready) on the SAME ses_…. The rewind itself is asserted ONLY as a negative invariant on the dwell window before turn 3 (stays ready; no new session). session_birth anchors to recording start UNPINNED so a transient proc-<PID> presession can't steal identity and cascade same_session_as failures; turn1_start (first working ⇒ the ses_… session) establishes identity and every later phase pins via same_session_as. HOW runs differ: assistant wording, per-turn tokens, sub-second timing (model variance). Stable structurally: three ready→working→ready arcs on ONE ses_… session, no new session ever created, a single process_exited, final state ready. RECORDABILITY: /undo is an in-TUI slash command and headless `opencode run` stores it as literal user text (confirmed this sweep with /model and /new); drive-opencode-interactive.sh has no slash-command step against a live held-open session, so this spec is the target a future live-REPL opencode driver must reproduce — not a currently-validated recording. daemon_capability assessed full but UNTESTED LIVE: the message-removal (transcript shrink then grow) is a store mutation no other opencode cell exercises; the daemon's re-read across a message-count DECREASE is the most likely observability_correction once captured.

text: A ses_… session appears in ready within 2s of agent launch (pre-session or ses_… UUID, whichever arrives first); never enters waiting before the first prompt
PID bound to the ses_… session
First prompt → working (binds the ses_… session; the pre-session never enters working)
First reply completes → back to ready; this is the checkpoint the user will rewind to
Second prompt → working again on the same ses_… session
Second reply completes → back to ready

User invokes /undo to rewind to the turn-1 checkpoint: the rewind happens in place on the same ses_… (opencode removes the subsequent messages from the SQLite session and restores the working-tree files via a git snapshot) — the session stays ready and no new session is minted; no state change distinguishes the rewind from a dwell
Third prompt, issued from the rewound point → working again on the SAME ses_… (in-place rewind, not a fork)
Post-rewind reply completes → back to ready; the turn arc is clean from the rewound checkpoint
Agent torn down once after the post-rewind turn → a single process_exited on the ses_… session; state immediately before teardown is ready
